message:
refactor(websocket): require explicit Cx for close handshake

WebSocket::close() previously used Cx::current() internally to get
the timer driver for close timeout tracking. This was fragile — if
no Cx was in scope, it fell back to wall_now(), and each timer check
repeated the Cx::current() lookup.

Both client and server close() now take &Cx explicitly:
- Timer lookup is hoisted into a closure captured once at the top
- No more repeated Cx::current() calls in the poll loop
- Caller must provide context, making the dependency visible

New test: close_uses_explicit_cx_and_closes_on_peer_eof verifies the
new signature works with a test Cx.

All websocket integration/conformance tests updated to pass &cx.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
